title: adenostoma fasciculatum var. fasciculatum
intro: Adenostoma fasciculatum var. fasciculatum, commonly known as red shank or sticky chaparral, is a shrub native to California and a member of the heath family (Ericaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, colds, and sore throats, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Adenostoma fasciculatum var. fasciculatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively studied.
